findings.title: Child lanes can bypass parent mutation authority by pausing/clearing and re-minting loop lease
findings.rationale: `pause_ralph_loop_lease` and `clear_ralph_loop_lease` are callable without validating `CODEX1_PARENT_LOOP_AUTHORITY_TOKEN`. Once a child lane pauses or clears the active lease, `begin_ralph_loop_lease` can mint a fresh parent authority token (its guard only blocks replacement of an active lease), and `validate_parent_loop_authority_for_mission_mutation` no longer protects the mission when no active lease is present. This allows a child lane to seize effective parent authority and execute protected mission-truth mutations, defeating the intended boundary.
findings.suggested_next_action: Require parent-loop authority token (or an explicit trusted parent override path) for `pause-loop-lease` and `clear-loop-lease` when a lease with `parent_authority_verifier` exists, and add regression tests proving child lanes without the token cannot pause/clear/re-mint authority before protected mutations.
